articles.title: k6 1.0: Go-Powered Load Testing with JavaScript
articles.description: A full-featured, configurable load generation tool that uses the Sobek Go-powered JavaScript engine to support writing test scripts in JavaScript. v1.0 promises stability, first-class TypeScript support, and better extensibility.
articles.author: Grafana Labs

articles.title: Node 24 (Current) Released
articles.description: Node’s release lines are shifting a little lately – v18 has gone EOL and now v23 gives way to v24 as the ‘Current’ release for when you need the cutting edge features. It comes with npm 11, V8 13.6 (hello RegExp.escape, Float16Array, and Error.isError), the URLPattern API exposed by default, plus Undici 7.
articles.author: Node.js Team

articles.title: Visual Studio Code Version 1.100
articles.description: Not to be confused with a 1.1 release, this update to VS Code has quite a few goodies for JavaScript developers with improved ‘Next Edit Suggestions’ that will suggest adding missing imports, support for Node’s enhanced network debugging capabilities, improved visibility of type information, remote MCP server support, GPT 4.1 as the new default base model, and more.
articles.author: Microsoft

articles.title: The Power of the Spread and Rest Syntax
articles.description: A quick run through the opportunities opened up with just three little dots ...
articles.author: Matt Smith

articles.title: React Server Components for Astro Developers
articles.description: Astro’s ‘island’ architecture and React Server Components share a strikingly similar mental model. Dan compares the two, digs into some of the quirks, and suggests Astro offers a ‘gentler onramp’ to some of RSC’s ideas if you’re struggling with them.
articles.author: Dan Abramov

articles.title: Migrating a Project from Prettier and ESLint to Biome
articles.description: Prettier and ESLint are staples in many JavaScript build processes, but Biome offers an interesting ‘one stop shop’ alternative.
articles.author: Damilola Olatunji

articles.title: HelloCSV: A Drop-In, CSV Importing Workflow for JS Apps
articles.description: If you or your users have CSV files to import, here’s a complete CSV importing workflow for the frontend that you can drop into your app. Basic docs.
articles.author: HelloCSV

articles.title: PptxGenJS 4.0: Build PowerPoint Presentations with JavaScript
articles.description: A mature library that outputs standards-compliant Open Office XML files compatible with PowerPoint, Apple Keynote, and other common presentation tools. Supports graphics, text, tables, and other typical slide objects. Lots of demos.
articles.author: Brent Ely

articles.title: Mantine 8.0: The Full Featured React Components Library
articles.description: Mantine is one of the most celebrated React components libraries, and for good reason: it’s full featured, modern, and looks great. v8.0 ups its chart game with a little Recharts magic, adds over twenty new components (including a GitHub-style Heatmap, Tree and SemiCircleProgress), submenus, and much more.
articles.author: Vitaly Rtishchev et al.

articles.title: Hyparquet: Parquet File Parser for JavaScript
articles.description: Parquet is a popular column-oriented data file format frequently used for storing large datasets for analysis. Hyparquet is a dependency-free JavaScript library for working with Parquet files, even in the browser (as in this demo).
articles.author: Hyperparam

articles.title: 🔊 react-sounds: Add Sound Effects to React Apps
articles.description: Having sound effects on the Web might sound like your idea of a nightmare, but this is a well put together project with delicate, well thought out examples that strike the right tone.
articles.author: Aedilic Inc.

articles.title: mono-jsx: <html> as a Response
articles.description: A server-side JSX runtime that renders <html> to a Response with no build step needed and works across numerous server-side JS runtimes.
articles.author: Je Xia

articles.description: The Node.js team is keen for you to take the latest Node.js Next 10 survey to help guide Node's future priorities.
Polycompiler uses a cute trick to let you merge Python and JavaScript into the same source file.
The OpenJS Foundation has announced its new board of directors.
🤖 ESLint v9.26.0 – An interesting release for the popular static analysis tool as it adds support for MCP as a way for ESLint to be used directly by AI models and coding agents.
🗾 Mapbox GL JS 3.12 – Interactive, customizable vector maps in the browser, rendered with WebGL.
Relay v19 – Facebook's declarative React/GraphQL framework.
Material UI 7.1 – React components using Material Design. Now Tailwind CSS 4 compatible
Rspack 1.3.9, Babylon.js 8.7, Electron 36 (official blog post)

articles.title: The Height Enigma
articles.description: A beginner-friendly no nonsense unraveling of the ‘mysterious’ behavior of percentage-based heights in CSS: “Like with so much in CSS, it feels random until you learn about the underlying mechanisms that explain the behaviour, and then it all makes perfect sense”. A great, visualized explainer.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: How to Have the Browser Pick a Contrasting Color in CSS
articles.description: Jen shares how the upcoming contrast-color() function should help with accessibility. It’s all in flux right now, but the takeaway being we should be able to declare a color in CSS and then have the browser figure out what should be paired with it — neat.
articles.author: Jen Simmons (WebKit)

articles.title: Add Wide Gamut P3 and Alpha Transparency to Your Color Picker in HTML
articles.description: Did you know that the native HTML <input type=color> picker recently got an upgrade and it now supports the full range of colors found in the P3 color gamut?
articles.author: Anne van Kesteren and Jen Simmons (WebKit)

articles.title: Integrating Localization Into Design Systems
articles.description: A case study that looks at how two designers tackled the challenges of building a localization-ready design system for a global audience, taking into consideration things such as text overflow, RTL layouts, font inconsistencies, and more.
articles.author: Rebecca Hemstad & Mark Malek

articles.title: Container Query for “Is There Enough Space Outside This Element?”
articles.description: A good, practical example of what container queries are useful for.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: It Wasn’t the Idea That Failed: It Was the Execution
articles.description: A look back at the WYSIWYG history of visual web development tools, from Visual Basic and FrontPage in the 90s to modern tools like the aforementioned Figma Sites. It argues that these tools often fail because they produce poor HTML markup, hindering accessibility and developer control. It then positions Nordcraft as an alternative, and yes it’s on the Nordcraft blog.
articles.author: Salma Alam-Naylor

articles.title: Creating Flower Shapes using clip-path: shape()
articles.description: Here's a detailed look at the what can be achieved when using the arc command. Plenty of code examples to help explain what's going on. Geometry, radius, and math problems a plenty here.
articles.author: Temani Afif

articles.title: Fonts Ninja: A Platform to Discover and Build Your Font Collection
articles.description: The font gallery lets you filter by shape, style, weight, price, etc., and the service also includes a Chrome extension so you can easily bookmark your favorites.
articles.author: Fonts Ninja

articles.title: Basecoat: shadcn/ui's Magic, But Minus React
articles.description: shadcn/ui is a popular suite of well designed React components, but if you’ve wanted to use them in other contexts too, Basecoat is for you. Basecoat ports the components to a more vanilla form you can use elsewhere.
articles.author: Ronan Berder

articles.title: tailwind-animations: Easy Animations for Your Tailwind Project
articles.description: You can mess around with them on this demo, which allows you to adjust the duration, delay, and “steps”.
articles.author: Miguel Ángel Durán

articles.title: HelloCSV: A Drop-In, CSV Importing Workflow for JS Apps
articles.description: If you or your users have CSV files to import, here’s a complete CSV importing workflow for the frontend that you can drop into your app. Basic docs.
articles.author: HelloCSV

articles.title: Flatlogic Templates: Free Web & Mobile Templates for Startups
articles.description: These were recently open-sourced by the company, as explained in this Reddit post. Includes 29 templates built with React, Vue, Angular, Bootstrap, and React Native. Some nice stuff here.
articles.author: Flatlogic

articles.title: MoodHue: Generate a Color Palette Based on Mood with AI
articles.description: Enter a ‘mood’ in the prompt and this will generate a 5-color palette from your description. There’s also an option to use a slider that shows the same colors as a blurry/mesh gradient.
articles.author: MoodHue

articles.title: Why We Are Still Using 88x31 Buttons
articles.description: You don't really see them anywhere near as much as you used to, but as a little image format they sure are cool and bring about a certain unique restrained creativity. This post, from last year, runs through how the specific size came to be, and how it endured.
articles.author: Brian (Ultrasciencelabs)
